LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT STARWEST BONTANICALS, INC.'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE IS DENIED. SAN FRANCISCO IS A PROPER VENUE, AS VIOLATIONS ALLEGEDLY OCCURRED HERE. AS TO CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES FOR TRIAL (IF A TRIAL OCCURS), DEFENDANT'S UTAH AND NEW MEXICO EMPLOYEES ARE EFFECTIVELY EQUIDISTANT FROM SAN FRANCISCO AND SACRAMENTO. FOR OTHER EMPLOYEES, SACRAMENTO IS LESS THAN 100 MILES FROM SAN FRANCISCO. ORDER AFTER HEARING SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. THE COURT ORDERS THAT DEFENDANT STARWEST BOTANICALS, INC. SHALL HAVE UNTIL APRIL 23, 2015 TO ANSWER IN THIS CASE. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/RBU)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-22-2017 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-13-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Erika McCartney's motion for entry of consent judgment is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements. Order and Judgment signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert B. Hancock, of Pacific Justice Center, Tel. 415.310.1940, for plaintiffs, appearing via CourtCall;
Daniel H. Dressman, of Gordon & Rees LLP, Tel. 510.463.8549, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. Defendant's counsel does not stipulate to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem. PLAINTIFF ERIKA MCCARTNEY Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition is continued to August 15, 2016, at 9:30 a.m., in Department 302, Law & Motion. Judge Pro Tem to prepare report and recommendation. Judge Pro Tem: Anthony Schoenberg; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition
Off Calendar Jul-19-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Duplicate entry.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2016 Continued To Sep-19-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-13-2015 Set For Trial On Mar-21-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Starwest Bontanicals, Inc.¿S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar Apr-09-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ROBERT B. HANCOCK of Pacific Justice Center, Tel No. (415) 310-1940 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. SAMUEL D. JUBELIRER of Gordon & Rees LLP, Tel No. (415) 986-5900 appearing for Defendant Starwest Botanicals, Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STARWEST BOTANICALS INC.'S Motion To Transfer Venue is DENIED. San Francisco is a proper venue, as violations allegedly occurred here. As to convenience of witnesses for trial (if a trial occurs), defendant's Utah and New Mexico employees are effectively equidistant from San Francisco and Sacramento. For other employees, Sacramento is less than 100 miles from San Francisco. ORDER AFTER HEARING SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. The Court orders that Defendant Starwest Botanicals, Inc. shall have until April 23, 2015 to answer in this case. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/RBU)
